chapter: Chapter: New Kings and Kingdoms (Our Pasts-II)

questions.question: How did a 'samanta' transition into becoming a 'maha-mandaleshvara'?

options.A: By being appointed by the king's council
options.B: By gaining power and wealth and declaring independence from their overlords
options.C: By marrying into the royal family
options.D: By building the largest temple in the kingdom

questions.explanation: As samantas gained power and wealth, they declared themselves to be maha-samanta or maha-mandaleshvara (the great lord of a circle or region) and sometimes asserted independence.

questions.question: The 'Hiranya-garbha' ritual performed by Dantidurga was significant because:

options.A: It allowed him to become a Brahmana
options.B: It was used to celebrate the birth of a prince
options.C: It allowed a non-Kshatriya by birth to be 'reborn' as a Kshatriya
options.D: It was a ritual to end a drought

questions.explanation: Dantidurga was a Rashtrakuta chief. By performing this ritual with the help of Brahmanas, he sought the status of a Kshatriya to legitimize his rule.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the 'Kadamba Mayurasharman' and 'Gurjara-Pratihara Harichandra'?

options.A: They were Kshatriyas who became Brahmanas
options.B: They were Brahmanas who gave up their traditional professions to take up arms
options.C: They were foreign invaders who settled in India
options.D: They were famous poets in the Chola court

questions.explanation: Both were Brahmanas who successfully established kingdoms in Karnataka and Rajasthan respectively after taking up arms.

questions.question: In the administration of medieval kingdoms, resources were often shared with:

options.C: The common people through welfare schemes

questions.explanation: Resources collected from producers were used to finance the king's establishment, build temples/forts, and were shared with Brahmanas.

questions.question: The Chola inscriptions mention 'Kadamai'. What does this term refer to?
questions.explanation: While 'vetti' was forced labor, 'Kadamai' was the term used for land revenue in the Chola inscriptions.

questions.question: Why are 'Prashastis' not considered 100% historically accurate by modern historians?

options.A: They were written in a language no one understands
options.B: They were written by enemies of the king
options.C: They often contain exaggerated praise to please the royal patron
options.D: They only talk about the weather

questions.explanation: Prashastis were composed by Brahmanas to depict kings as valiant and victorious; they often contain poetic exaggerations rather than objective facts.

questions.question: What made Kalhana’s 'Rajatarangini' different from traditional Prashastis?

options.A: It was written in Prakrit, not Sanskrit
options.B: It was critical of rulers and their policies
options.C: It was written by a king himself
options.D: It did not mention any wars

questions.explanation: Unlike Prashasti writers, Kalhana used a variety of sources and was often critical about rulers and their policies, making his work unique for its time.

questions.question: The 'Tripartite Struggle' was a long-drawn conflict between which three powers?
questions.explanation: These three dynasties fought for centuries to control the prestigious city of Kanauj in the Ganga valley.

questions.question: Why did medieval rulers primarily target temples during their military campaigns?

options.A: To convert the population to a new religion
options.B: Because temples were extremely wealthy and symbolized the king's power
options.C: Because temples were used as military barracks
options.D: To destroy the historical records kept there

questions.explanation: Kings demonstrated their power and wealth by building large temples; therefore, when they attacked each other, they targeted these wealthy symbols.

questions.question: Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni was particularly interested in finding out more about the people he conquered. Who did he entrust with this task?
questions.explanation: Al-Biruni wrote the 'Kitab-al-Hind', which remains an important source for historians today.

questions.question: Prithviraja III was defeated by Sultan Muhammad Ghori in which year?
questions.explanation: Prithviraja III won the first battle in 1191, but lost to Ghori in the very next year, 1192.

questions.question: The rise of the Cholas began when Vijayalaya captured the Kaveri delta from the ______.
questions.explanation: Vijayalaya captured the delta from the Muttaraiyar family, who were subordinates to the Pallava kings of Kanchipuram.

questions.question: Who is considered the most powerful Chola ruler?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I became king in 985 and expanded Chola control over most of the regions mentioned in the chapter.

questions.question: Rajendra I, the son of Rajaraja I, is famous for his naval expedition to:
questions.explanation: Rajendra I developed a powerful navy and raided the Ganga valley, Sri Lanka, and countries of Southeast Asia.

questions.question: The big temples of Thanjavur and Gangaikonda-cholapuram were built by:
questions.explanation: These temples are architectural and sculptural marvels built during the peak of Chola power.

questions.question: Besides being places of worship, Chola temples were also centers of:

options.B: Craft production, social, and economic life

questions.explanation: Temples were nuclei of settlements and were endowed with land, supporting many people like priests, garland makers, cooks, and dancers.

questions.question: How did the Cholas manage the irrigation required for agriculture in the Kaveri delta?

options.A: By importing water from the north
options.B: By building embankments and digging canals and wells

questions.explanation: The Kaveri river floods frequently; the Cholas built embankments to prevent flooding and canals to carry water to the fields.

questions.question: In the Chola administration, what was a 'Nadu'?
questions.explanation: A group of peasant settlements (ur) formed larger units called 'Nadu', which performed administrative functions like justice and tax collection.

questions.question: Rich peasants of the ______ caste exercised considerable control over the affairs of the 'nadu'.
questions.explanation: The Vellala caste peasants supervised the functions of the nadu under the oversight of the central Chola government.

questions.question: What title did Chola kings give to rich landowners as a mark of respect?
questions.explanation: Titles like Muvendavelan (a velan or peasant serving three kings) and Araiyar (chief) were given to rich landowners.

questions.question: A 'Sabha' in Chola administration was an assembly of:
questions.explanation: The Sabha looked after the Brahmadeya (land gifted to Brahmanas) and worked through separate committees.

questions.question: According to the Uttaramerur inscription, how were members of committees in the Sabha chosen?

options.B: By drawing lots (slips of palm leaf in an earthenware pot)

questions.explanation: Names of eligible persons were written on palm leaves, put in a pot, and a young boy was asked to pick the slips.

questions.question: The term 'Vellanvagai' refers to:

options.A: Land for maintenance of a school

questions.explanation: Chola inscriptions categorize different types of land; Vellanvagai was for non-Brahmana peasants.

questions.question: What was the function of the 'Nagaram'?

options.B: Associations of traders that performed administrative functions in towns
options.C: A group of priests in charge of education

questions.explanation: Nagarams were organizations of traders that occasionally performed administrative tasks in urban areas.

questions.question: Which river delta was the center of Chola power?
questions.explanation: The Kaveri delta was the heart of the Chola kingdom, providing fertile land for the expansion of their empire.
